name: Holt Primary Process Scoring System
source: Holt, R.R. (2009). Primary Process Thinking: Theory, Measurement, and Research. Jason Aronson.

formal_categories.name: Condensation
formal_categories.definition: Two or more distinct ideas, images, or percepts merged into a single representation
formal_categories.example: A composite figure combining features of two different things

formal_categories.name: Displacement
formal_categories.definition: An attribute, affect, or quality shifted from one idea to another in an associative network; one thing comes to stand for another

formal_categories.name: Symbolism
formal_categories.definition: Use of conventional or idiosyncratic symbols to represent drive-laden content

formal_categories.name: Contradiction/Inconsistency
formal_categories.definition: Tolerance of logical contradictions within a single response (something simultaneously alive and dead, present and absent)

formal_categories.name: Autistic Logic
formal_categories.definition: Reasoning that follows private, idiosyncratic rules rather than conventional logic

formal_categories.name: Image Fusion/Fluidity
formal_categories.definition: Percepts that shift, merge, or transform in unstable ways

formal_categories.name: Loose Associations
formal_categories.definition: Ideas connected by superficial, tangential, or clang associations rather than logical relationships

defense_demand.description: Index of the intensity or raw quality of the primary process manifestation — how much pressure the response places on the ego's defenses
defense_demand.scale_note: Rated from low to high (typically 1-5 or 1-7 depending on version)
defense_demand.low: Content is mild, well-disguised, socially acceptable (Level 2 content, mild formal deviations)
defense_demand.high: Content is raw, blatant, direct, socially unacceptable (Level 1 content, severe formal deviations)

defense_effectiveness.description: Estimate of the cognitive integration of primary process content — how well the ego manages, controls, or contextualizes the material
defense_effectiveness.poor: Material overwhelms the response; poor form quality, anxiety, loss of distance, inability to frame adaptively; person is flooded by the material
defense_effectiveness.good: Material is well-integrated; good form quality, appropriate distance, humor, creativity, or artistic use; adaptive regression in the service of the ego

percent_pp.description: Proportion of all responses containing scorable primary process content or formal deviations

mean_dd.description: Average Defense Demand across all responses containing primary process material

mean_de.description: Average Defense Effectiveness across all responses containing primary process material

rego.name: Adaptive Regression Score (Regression in the Service of the Ego)
rego.description: Composite index combining PP amount, intensity (DD), and management (DE). High REGO = primary process present but well-controlled (adaptive/creative). Low REGO = primary process present and poorly controlled (maladaptive/pathological).

clinical_interpretation.adaptive_regression: High DE + moderate PP: Associated with creativity, artistic achievement, tolerance of ambiguity
clinical_interpretation.maladaptive_regression: Low DE + high PP: Associated with thought disorder, poor reality testing, flooding by drive material
clinical_interpretation.key_distinction: It is not the presence of primary process that is pathological, but the inability to control it
